svg.title: Chapter 12: the three professional-tier AWS certifications and the step up in scope, duration, and question style from the associate tier
svg.desc: A lower band shows the associate tier as the assumed starting point, with a note that no formal prerequisite exists but roughly two years of hands-on AWS experience is expected. Three professional certifications sit above it. Solutions Architect Professional (SAP-C02) tests multi-account, multi-Region architecture and migration at organizational scale, drawing on Chapters 2, 3, 6, and 7. DevOps Engineer Professional (DOP-C02) tests continuous delivery, automation, and operational resilience, drawing on Chapters 6 and 7. Generative AI Developer Professional (AIP-C01) is the newest professional exam and tests building and deploying production generative-AI solutions on services such as Amazon Bedrock; it reaches beyond this volume. All three run 180 minutes with 75 questions and are dominated by long, multi-paragraph scenario questions rather than recall.
svg.text: Chapter 12: The Professional Tier — Scope, Not Just Difficulty
180 minutes / 75 questions, dominated by long scenario items — the jump is in breadth and reading load, not obscurity
no formal prerequisite — but ~2 years hands-on AWS is assumed
Associate tier — the practical starting point (Chapter 11)
AWS removed the old associate-before-professional requirement years ago; the experience gap remains real regardless
What actually changes at this tier
• Reading load. Scenario stems run several paragraphs; time pressure comes from reading speed, not recall speed.
• Trade-offs over facts. Several answers work; the question is which best fits stated cost, RTO/RPO, and compliance constraints.
• Breadth across services. One question can span identity, networking, storage, and cost governance at once.
• Organizational scale. The unit of design is the organization and its accounts, not a single workload.
